perturbation.original_text: mPhase agrees to abide by the guidelines appearing on Schedule B of this Agreement specifying the dimensions, requirements and specifications, and the review process related to use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo. mPhase understands that these guidelines may be updated from time to time and agrees to abide by those updates as well as the guidelines provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: mPhase agrees to generally consider the guidelines related to use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo. mPhase understands that these guidelines may be updated from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'specifying the dimensions, requirements and specifications, and the review process', the clause becomes vague. Although mPhase still agrees to 'generally consider' the guidelines, the specifics are gone, creating uncertainty in enforcement. This change contradicts section 9, which mentions that Lucent has the right to 'inspect the Approved Uses bearing the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, to ensure that those uses reviewed and approved pursuant to the procedure set forth in Schedule B continue to be in compliance with the terms of this Agreement' - but there's no review process explicitly defined in Section 2, impacting the implementation of section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: mPhase agrees to allow Lucent to inspect the Approved Uses bearing the Lucent Co-Branding Logo, to ensure that those uses reviewed and approved pursuant to the procedure set forth in Schedule B continue to be in compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit requirements and specifications tied to Schedule B, which directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' that relies on the procedures defined in Schedule B for Lucent to inspect 'Approved Uses' and ensure compliance. The original text mandated adherence to specific guidelines and a review process, whereas the changed text only requires general consideration, making the inspection and compliance assurance outlined in the 'Contradicted Text' unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that mPhase becomes aware of any unauthorized use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo or other Lucent marks by third parties, mPhase agrees to promptly notify Lucent and to cooperate fully, at Lucent's expense, in any enforcement of Lucent's rights against such third Source: MPHASE TECHNOLOGIES INC, 10-K, 9/11/2003 parties. Nothing contained in this paragraph shall be construed to require Lucent to enforce any rights against third parties or to restrict Lucent's rights to license or consent to such third parties' use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo or any other Lucent marks.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that mPhase becomes aware of any unauthorized use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo or other Lucent marks by third parties, mPhase agrees to promptly notify Lucent. Nothing contained in this paragraph shall be construed to require Lucent to enforce any rights against third parties or to restrict Lucent's rights to license or consent to such third parties' use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo or any other Lucent marks.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'and to cooperate fully, at Lucent's expense, in any enforcement of Lucent's rights against such third parties' has been removed. This creates a contradiction: mPhase is still obligated to notify Lucent of unauthorized use, but their responsibility to assist in enforcing Lucent's rights is eliminated, creating an ambiguity. It contradicts the general intention of protecting Lucent's brand, implying a level of uncooperativeness.
perturbation.contradicted_text: mPhase agrees that it acquires no rights to the Lucent Co-Branding Logo nor to the marks LUCENT, LUCENT TECHNOLOGIES or LUCENT INNOVATION RING DESIGN, by its use and that any use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo by mPhase inures to the benefit of Lucent.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that mPhase must 'cooperate fully, at Lucent's expense, in any enforcement of Lucent's rights against such third parties'. The changed text omits this obligation. The contradicted text states 'mPhase agrees that it acquires no rights to the Lucent Co-Branding Logo nor to the marks LUCENT... by its use and that any use of the Lucent Co-Branding Logo by mPhase inures to the benefit of Lucent.' By removing mPhase's obligation to cooperate in enforcing Lucent's rights, it introduces a contradiction because while mPhase's use of the logo benefits Lucent and mPhase has no rights to it, the changed text allows mPhase to be less cooperative in protecting the logo, hindering Lucent's benefit from mPhase's use, and creating an inconsistency in the agreement's overall intent and enforceability. This fulfills the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition because essential language regarding cooperation was removed, creating a contradiction with the obligation of mPhase ensuring that Lucent benefits from the use of the Co-Branding logo.

perturbation.original_text: mPhase shall have the right to annually renew this agreement for a period of one year upon each annual expiration with the written consent of Lucent, which written consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: mPhase shall have the option to renew this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific condition that 'written consent shall not be unreasonably withheld,' the renewal process becomes much more uncertain. The original text placed a constraint on Lucent's decision-making, whereas the changed text grants Lucent unrestricted power over renewal, which is contradictory with the original intent to allow renewal, creating a power imbalance which is not clarified in the changed text. This change can cause a problem when linked to section 11. which states the agreement should continue for a term of one (1) year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Term of this Agreement will commence on the date above, and shall continue for a term of one (1) year. mPhase shall have the right to annually renew this agreement for a period of one year upon each annual expiration with the written consent of Lucent, which written consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Either party wishing to terminate the Agreement must give written notice to the other party at least thirty (30) days prior to the desired date of termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that Lucent's consent for renewal 'shall not be unreasonably withheld.' This places a clear limitation on Lucent's ability to deny renewal. The changed text removes this limitation, giving Lucent unrestricted power over renewal. This directly contradicts the original intention, creating uncertainty about the renewal process and potentially rendering the one-year term stated in the contradicted text ineffective because Lucent can choose not to renew the agreement, rendering the one-year term potentially meaningless.
